title: Vegetable Cutlet

overview.description: Crisp, golden Indian vegetable cutlets made from mashed potato and spiced vegetables with a crunchy breadcrumb coating.

ingredients: 500g potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
½ tsp chilli powder, or to taste
method: Boil the potatoes in salted water until tender.
Drain thoroughly and allow them to steam dry.
Mash the potatoes until smooth.
Cook the carrots, green beans and peas until tender but not soft.
Drain thoroughly.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a frying pan.
Add the onion and cook until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric and chilli powder.
Cook for 30 seconds.
Add the cooked vegetables and stir thoroughly.
Cook for 2–3 minutes to remove excess moisture.
Add the vegetable mixture to the mashed potatoes.
Add the lemon juice, salt, black pepper and fresh coriander.
Mix thoroughly.
Add the breadcrumbs and mix until the mixture holds together when pressed.
Shape the mixture into 8–10 oval or round cutlets.
Mix the plain flour with enough water to make a thin batter.
Dip each cutlet into the batter.
Coat thoroughly with breadcrumbs.
Chill the cutlets for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a thin layer of oil in a frying pan over medium heat.
Fry the cutlets in batches until golden brown and crisp on both sides.
Drain briefly before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Vegetable cutlets are found in several South Asian cuisines and are particularly popular as snacks, appetisers and tea-time food.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a vegetable mixture that holds together, how to manage moisture and how to create a crisp breadcrumb coating.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive, freezer-friendly vegetable snack that uses common vegetables and can be served as a snack, starter or light meal.
chapman-tips: Allow the potatoes to steam dry after boiling so the mixture does not become wet.
Cook the vegetables until tender but avoid overcooking them.
Cook the vegetable mixture briefly after adding the spices to remove excess moisture.
The mixture should hold together firmly when pressed.
Chilling the shaped cutlets makes them much easier to coat and fry.
Don't make the cutlets too thick or the centres may remain cold when the coating is golden.
Use a mixture of vegetables for better texture.
The cutlets can be shaped smaller for children's snacks or party food.
Fry in batches to avoid lowering the oil temperature too much.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Freeze the shaped and breadcrumb-coated cutlets before frying, then cook from thawed or partially thawed.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in an oven or air fryer until hot and crisp.
